migrations.description: Lift Client URI list attributes from StringArray to RecordArray<ClientUriEntry> (AB#4209 Step 1)

scripts.description:
Lift Client URI list attributes from StringArray to RecordArray<ClientUriEntry>.

Phase 3 follow-up (AB#4209): the three URI lists on Client — RedirectUris,
PostLogoutRedirectUris, AllowedCorsOrigins — become record lists so each entry
can carry a Source marker. Existing scalar URI entries become records with
Source = "base" (i.e. they were managed by the blueprint seed and may be
rewritten by the cleanup gate on the next re-apply). REST-API-added URIs going
forward carry Source = "api"; overlay cmdlets write Source = "overlay:<name>".

See the concept doc at
octo-platform-services/docs/concepts/phase-3-followup-identity-local-dev-overlay.md
§4.5 for the full source taxonomy.

steps.description: Lift Client.RedirectUris scalar entries into ClientUriEntry records (Source=base)
Lift Client.PostLogoutRedirectUris scalar entries into ClientUriEntry records (Source=base)
Lift Client.AllowedCorsOrigins scalar entries into ClientUriEntry records (Source=base)

types.description: Tracks a ClientCredentials client that the parent tenant has auto-provisioned into a child tenant. Lives in the parent tenant's database. One row per (parentClientId × childTenantId) pair.
ASP.NET Data Protection key-ring element. Service-global data persisted in the system tenant so every identity pod shares one key ring (replaces the file-system/PVC key store).
Maps an email domain pattern to a group for automatic group assignment on user login.
Maps a user from a parent tenant to roles in this (child) tenant.
A group that can be assigned roles. Users and other groups can be members.
Identity provider that delegates authentication to a parent tenant.
Duende IdentityServer server-side session. Holds the data-protected authentication ticket server-side so the per-tenant browser cookie only carries a session key (fixes multi-KB cookie bloat).

associationRoles.description: Links an entity (User, Client or Group) to an assigned Role.
Links a parent Group to a nested child Group.
Links a Group to its member entities (Users, Clients or ExternalTenantUserMappings).

attributes.description: Maximum lifetime of a refresh token in seconds. Defaults to 2592000 seconds / 30 days
The number of failed login attempts for the current user.
Lifetime of access token in seconds (defaults to 3600 seconds / 1 hour)
Specifies whether the access token is a reference token or a self contained JWT token (defaults to Jwt).
Controls whether access tokens are transmitted via the browser for this client
Specifies whether this client can request refresh tokens (defaults to false)
Specifies whether a proof key can be sent using plain method (not recommended and defaults to false).
Specifies whether user can choose to store consent decisions (defaults to true)
Controls whether new users can self-register via this identity provider. When false, only existing users can authenticate.
Signing algorithm for access token. If empty, will use the server default signing algorithm.
Gets or sets the allowed CORS origins for JavaScript clients. Each entry carries a Source marker (see RedirectUris).
Specifies the allowed grant types (legal combinations of AuthorizationCode, Implicit, Hybrid, ResourceOwner, ClientCredentials). Defaults to Implicit.
Signing algorithm for identity token. If empty, will use the server default signing algorithm.
Specifies the api scopes that the client is allowed to request. If empty, the client can't access any scope
When requesting both an id token and access token, should the user claims always be added to the id token instead of requiring the client to use the userinfo endpoint.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether client claims should be always included in the access tokens - or only for client credentials flow.
Gets or sets the URI of the provider, or discovery endpoint for OpenID Connect discovery.
Lifetime of authorization code in seconds (defaults to 300 seconds / 5 minutes)

When true, the client is auto-provisioned (mirrored) into every new child tenant of the tenant it lives in. Enables a single client identity (e.g. CI/CD service) to reach many tenants without per-tenant manual setup.
Specifies if the user's session id should be sent to the BackChannelLogoutUri. Defaults to true.
Specifies logout URI at client for HTTP back-channel based logout.
The tenant ID of the child tenant in a parent-child mirror relationship.
The backchannel authentication request lifetime in seconds.
The type of the claim.
The value of the claim.
The value type of the claim.
Gets or sets the client claims to be sent to the user info endpoint.
Gets or sets a value to prefix it on client claim types. Defaults to client_.
Gets or sets the client secret of the application.
URI to further information about client (used on consent screen)
Lifetime of a user consent in seconds. Defaults to null (no expiration)
When enabled, the client's token lifetimes (e.g. refresh tokens) will be tied to the user's session lifetime.
Clock skew used in validating the client's DPoP proof token 'iat' claim value. Defaults to 5 minutes.
Enum setting to control validation for the DPoP proof token expiration.
Optional RtId of a group to which new users are automatically added on first login via this provider.
Specifies the lifetime (in seconds) of the device code. Defaults to 300 seconds / 5 minutes.
Optional display name of the user owning the session.
The email address of the user.
Indicates if the email address of the user has been confirmed.
Email domain pattern to match (e.g., "meshmakers.com"). Matched against the domain part of the user's email.
Specifies if this client can use local accounts. Defaults to true.
The first name of the user.
Friendly name of an ASP.NET Data Protection key-ring element (format key-{guid}).
Specifies if the user's session id should be sent to the FrontChannelLogoutUri. Defaults to true.
Specifies logout URI at client for HTTP front-channel based logout.
An optional description of the group's purpose.
The display name of the group.
Gets or sets the host address of the identity provider.
Specifies which external IdPs can be used with this client (if list is empty all IdPs are allowed). Defaults to empty.
Lifetime of identity token in seconds (defaults to 300 seconds / 5 minutes)
Value indicating whether JWT access tokens should include an identifier. Defaults to true.
Gets of sets a URI that can be used to initiate login from the IdentityServer host or a third party.
Specifies whether the consent screen will emphasize this scope (if the consent screen wants to implement such a feature). Defaults to false.
Specifies whether the user can de-select the scope on the consent screen (if the consent screen wants to implement such a feature). Defaults to false.
The last name of the user.
Flag indicating if the user could be locked out.
The date and time, in UTC, when any user lockout ends.
URI to client logo (used on consent screen)
The role IDs assigned to the mapped user in this tenant.
The normalized email address of the user.
The normalized (uppercase) group name for case-insensitive lookup.
The normalized name.
The normalized user name.
Gets or sets a salt value used in pair-wise subjectId generation for users of this client.
The tenant ID of the parent tenant that provides user authentication.
The salted and hashed representation of the password for this user.
The phone number of the user.
Indicates if the phone number of the user has been confirmed.
The backchannel authentication request polling interval in seconds.
Gets or sets the host port of the identity provider.
Specifies allowed URIs to redirect to after logout. Each entry carries a Source marker (see RedirectUris).
UTC timestamp when the mirror was provisioned.

When set on a Client living in a child tenant, marks this record as a mirror of a parent-tenant client identified by the given tenant id. Sub-tenant admins surface this as a read-only "Provisioned by parent tenant" indicator; the operator cannot edit the client locally — changes must go through the parent.
Specifies allowed URIs to return tokens or authorization codes to. Each entry carries a Source marker so local-dev / operator overlay URIs can coexist with base (blueprint) URIs.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether the access token (and its claims) should be updated on a refresh token request.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether the refresh token should be updated on a refresh token request.
UTC timestamp of the last sliding-expiration renewal of the session.
Specifies whether a consent screen is required. Defaults to false.
Specifies whether a DPoP (Demonstrating Proof-of-Possession) token is required to be used by this client (defaults to false)
Specifies whether a proof key is required for authorization code based token requests (defaults to true).
Specifies whether the client must use a request object on authorize requests (defaults to false).
Force the user to change the password after the next login.
The claims of a role.
The id of roles the user is a member of.
The ASP.NET authentication scheme that produced the session ticket.
Models the scopes this API resource allows.
Monotonic counter incremented on every secret rotation of the parent client. Used to detect mirrors that fell behind on a rotation.
A random value that should change whenever a users credentials have been compromised.
Unique key of a Duende server-side session (the only value stored in the browser cookie).
Gets or sets a value indicating whether the client will be shown in the discovery document. Defaults to true.
Sliding lifetime of a refresh token in seconds. Defaults to 1296000 seconds / 15 days

Provenance marker on a ClientUriEntry. "base" entries were authored by the blueprint seed (or by the cross-service identity-bootstrap consumer) and get rewritten on every blueprint re-apply. "api" entries were added by an operator through the public REST API and survive re-applies. "overlay:<name>" entries were added by a local-dev or operator overlay cmdlet and also survive re-applies; they are filtered out of `DumpTenant --clean` exports while base + api survive the export.
The tenant ID where the user's account resides.
The RtId of the user in the source tenant.
The username for display purposes.
RtId of the group to which matching users are added.
The serialized, data-protected ASP.NET authentication ticket of a server-side session.
Indicates if two factor authentication is enabled for the user.
Indicates whether the access token (and its claims) should be updated on a refresh token request.
A single URI value carried by a ClientUriEntry record.
Gets or sets if the connection to the identity provider should use TLS. Defaults to true.
Gets or sets the base DN of the user.
The claims of the user.
Specifies the user code type for the device flow. Defaults to null.
The existing logins of the user.
The user name.
Gets or sets the name of the user attribute.
Lifetime of user's single sign-on session (in seconds). Defaults to null (no expiration).
The tokens of the user.
The raw XML payload of a Data Protection key-ring element.
